title: Secure Reference v1
description: Frontmatter of a secure_refs/<id>.md pointer file (privacy_and_security.md pattern). A secure ref is a REFERENCE PRIMITIVE, never a secret container: it stores metadata plus an opaque locator into external secure storage. The document bytes never enter the workspace, and the pointer file must have NO body content. Validated as a separate validate_notes pass (OQ #28).

id.description: Canonical secure-ref id (SECURE_REF_PATTERN). Notes cite this id; it is not a ULID and never appears as an event subject_id.

kind.description: Document category, e.g. identity_document, bank_statement, lease. Metadata only — never the document's contents.

location.description: Always 'external': the bytes live in external secure storage, never in this workspace or repo.

locator.description: Opaque pointer into external secure storage (e.g. a keychain item name or encrypted-volume path). MUST NOT contain the secret/document content itself.

notes.description: Optional one-line metadata annotation. Never document contents.
